howItWorks.intro: This geothermal savings calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Cost = System Cost - (System Cost × Tax Credit%)
Annual Savings = Current Bill - Geothermal Bill
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How much does geothermal save on heating and cooling?
faq.answer: Geothermal heat pumps typically reduce heating and cooling costs by 50-70%. With a COP of 4.0, you get 4 units of heating for every 1 unit of electricity — 3-4× more efficient than conventional systems.

faq.question: How much does a geothermal system cost?
faq.answer: Residential geothermal: $15,000-$35,000 installed, depending on home size and loop type. The 30% federal tax credit (through 2032) brings this down significantly. Systems last 25-50 years.

faq.question: What is COP (Coefficient of Performance)?
faq.answer: COP measures heat pump efficiency: heat output ÷ electrical input. A COP of 4.0 means 400% efficiency — 4 units of heat per 1 unit of electricity. Geothermal COP ranges from 3.5-5.0.

howWeCalculate.intro: This geothermal savings calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
